reasoning: The Lean statement defines T_inf matching the informal definition: `Set.Infinite tᶜ` corresponds to "X - U is infinite", `t = ∅` corresponds to "X - U is all of X", and `t = Set.univ` corresponds to "X - U is empty" (disjuncts reordered, but ∨ is commutative). The three negated conditions (`univ ∈ T_inf`, closure under arbitrary unions, closure under binary intersections) exactly match Mathlib's TopologicalSpace axioms; `∅ ∈ T_inf` is automatic from the T_inf definition and also follows from the sUnion axiom on the empty family, so it is not needed separately. The existential `∃ X : Type, ¬(...)` correctly encodes "does not need to be a topology on X" (i.e., there exists an X for which the topology axioms fail). The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
